message:
fix(distributed): widen max_source_blocks to u16 and validate object identity + SBN range in recovery

EncodingConfig::max_source_blocks widened from u8 to u16 to support up
to 256 source blocks (the maximum representable by the u8 SBN field).
Previously u8 max capped at 255, preventing configs that needed exactly
256 blocks. Test added: encode_allows_full_256_block_boundary_via_config
constructs a snapshot that requires exactly 256 blocks and verifies
symbols span SBN 0..=255.

RecoveryCollector::add_collected_with_verify now validates:
1. The symbol's object_id matches the recovery target before dedup,
   rejecting foreign-object symbols with ErrorKind::CorruptedSymbol
2. The symbol's SBN is within the declared source_blocks range,
   rejecting out-of-range block indices

These checks run before the ESI range check and dedup lookup, preventing
a foreign-object symbol from evicting a legitimate symbol in the dedup
map. Tests added for foreign-object rejection and high-SBN (255)
acceptance at the 256-block boundary.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
